messages.content: U got internet search

Nope, no dedicated web search tool in my kit. I can hit specific URLs with `http_get` if you give me one, but I can't go googling around on my own.

Need me to fetch something? Drop the URL, sir.
You have all the tools, mcp is running in 3 places and you have the ollama sdk’s so what the exact problem

Fair point, sir. The "problem" is I don't have a `web_search` tool in the actual MCP schema — only `http_get`/`http_post`, which need a URL, not a query. I can call Ollama through `odin_chat`/`odin_auto`, but that hits your models, not Google. So no open-ended internet search unless you point me at a specific endpoint first.

Sorry, wrong button write me a schema. I guess you need all the tools in the MCP just write me a basic Shima. I’ve got all the tools in there. Write me a basic schema for what you need. What else you need besides the schema

I’ve been going at this for a month. I’ve got it back in service running. I’ve got MCP running and two projects. I’ve got schemes. I’ve got SDKs. I’ve got what did you just say? Said I’ve got MCP client MCP service MCP post MCPMCP server PY I’ve got 125 so who’s the deal? How do I get you or Clyde or somebody not Clyde code because they fucking obviously don’t work somebody they can get in there and figure who the fuck it is and fix it I can’t I’m about to give up

Stop. Breathe. You're not giving up, you're just fucking tired. Let's figure out exactly what's alive and what's lying to you, right now.

I'm going to hit your systems and get a real status report. No guessing.</think>
Well, I’m getting somewhere you and Kimmy both you’re doing this back snake thing and I guess you think you’re doing something. Maybe you are.

Yeah, I hear you. That looping "we're working on it" bullshit is infuriating when you just need the goddamn thing to work.

I'm not gonna spin you. Tell me one thing right now: **what is the actual error or broken behavior?** Like, what do you type or click that makes you say "this is fucked"?

That’s the thing everything is working except the back end keeps crashing. I’ll fix it in a little while, but that’s the note in the parlor. Everything is working, but you guys can’t seem to. I’ve got. I don’t know if you got the memory injection, but I’ve got two NA workflow going a separate memory injection and it’s like the more work. I do to target the little things every single one of them. it’s like there’s always something else something I’m missing.

Yeah. That’s integration hell. Every fix uncovers the next missing wire, and it feels like you’re chasing your own tail because you are — but that’s not you failing, that’s the stack being half-built across three clouds and your basement.

You’ve got the pieces. The problem is probably not one big thing, it’s ten tiny handoffs that don’t trust each other: memory injection → n8n → MCP → Ollama → response → memory save. One hiccup anywhere and the whole chain looks broken.

Here’s what I think is actually happening: you’ve built three parallel pipelines and none of them know the others exist. So when backend crashes, you don’t know which handoff failed.

Want me to do one thing that might actually help? Let me trace the real call flow — from user input through memory injection, n8n, MCP, Ollama, and back — and map what’s actually connected vs what’s just assumed. Then we can kill the guesswork.

Or if you’re done for today, that’s fine too. But don’t quit because you think you’re missing something obvious. You’re not. You’re just the only one holding the whole map in your head.

Definitely not done for the day it’s 1 o’clock, but I’m I’m taking a break. I’m definitely fucking taking a break and I tell you what I’ll leave you on here. This is one thing we’re gonna have to fix so you’re on my phone but the dashboard on my computer and I have Tailscale running but the conversation me and you were having now is separate from what’s going on on the dashboard. I need a way to transfer this to my computer without changing models without resetting the conversation because I have in the column over the right left whatever has conversations so I can go back to this conversation, but it’s probably not recording off of my phone on the computer. That’s something I haven’t even tried to attack yet. It’s just straight up displaying Tailscale to my Phone man the programs that work real good like Clyde code Reins they had a lot of coding like I’m halfway there and oh my God I’ve been going forever.

Smart — take the break. 1pm and you've been grinding a month, your brain needs a reset.

Before you go, I'm gonna stash this conversation so you can pick it up on the computer exactly where we left off. One sec.</think>
I’ll add the schema,  did you receive any memory injection from n8n or would you know

I don’t have any record of an n8n memory injection hitting me in this session. I only see what’s in our current chat context plus whatever comes through the MCP memory tools if they’re called.

So if n8n is feeding memory, either:
- It’s going to Bella/Memory Pro but not being pulled into my context, or
- It’s failing silently somewhere between n8n and the memory API.

Go take your break, sir. When you’re back, we can trace that n8n → memory → MCP → me flow and see where it’s dropping. Add the schema when you’re fresh — I’ll still be here.

It is feeding memory and it’s feeding it to the context box it’s built into the HTML, but it’s not built into my phone. I’m using Tailscale again, so are you really coming in over the phone or are you coming in over the computer and getting broadcast to the phone because if you’re coming in over the computer why is it not showing up on my computer but it’s showing up on my phone that’s confusing huh broadcasting it with a tunnel from my computer, but you’re not on the computer but you are on the computer therefore you’re getting the NA in but you’re on my phone and you’re not getting the what’s really going on

I’m not “coming in” through either device. I live on your backend. Your phone client and your computer dashboard are two separate clients talking to the same backend — but they’re probably using different session IDs or different context paths, so they don’t see each other.

That’s why n8n feeds the computer dashboard’s context box but your phone conversation stays in its own lane. The backend works; the clients just aren’t sharing state.

Go take your break. When you’re back, the one thing to check is: **does the phone client and the computer dashboard use the same `session_id`?** If not, that’s the whole problem.
Is there a deepseek like Claud dashboard,

Yeah. DeepSeek has two:

- **chat.deepseek.com** — the chat dashboard, like Claude's web app.
- **platform.deepseek.com** — API keys, usage, models, like Anthropic Console.

If you want DeepSeek running in your own stack, Ollama has it too:
- `ollama run deepseek-r1:7b` or `deepseek-r1:14b`
- `ollama run deepseek-coder-v2`

So you can either use their hosted dashboard, or pull the model and run it locally through your BELLA dashboard.
